metadata.description: Structural Causal Model for David Chalmers' Naturalistic Dualism, formalizing the Hard Problem Gap, zombie argument, natural supervenience, and Information Space dual-aspect theory

nodes.name: Physical Domain
nodes.label: P (Physical States)
nodes.description: The set of all physical states and properties, including neural correlates and brain activity. Causally closed domain governed by physical laws L_P.

nodes.name: Psychological Domain
nodes.label: M (Functional/Behavioral States)
nodes.description: The set of all functional and behavioral states defined by causal roles. Includes beliefs, desires, judgments. Logically supervenes on P.

nodes.name: Phenomenal Domain
nodes.label: E (Consciousness/Qualia)
nodes.description: The set of all experiential/qualitative states (qualia). What it is like to have an experience. Ontologically fundamental, irreducible to P or M.

nodes.name: Fundamental Laws
nodes.label: L = L_P ∪ L_Ψ
nodes.description: The complete set of fundamental laws governing the universe. Union of physical laws (L_P) and psychophysical laws (L_Ψ).

nodes.name: Physical Laws
nodes.label: L_P (Physics)
nodes.description: Physical laws governing domain P. Sufficient for all physical causation. Does NOT include psychophysical bridging laws.

nodes.name: Psychophysical Laws
nodes.label: L_Ψ (Bridging Laws)
nodes.description: Fundamental psychophysical laws that bridge P and E. Cannot be derived from L_P alone. Required for natural supervenience.

nodes.name: Information Space
nodes.label: I (Informational States)
nodes.description: The set of all informational states. Neutral monist substrate with dual realizations (physical and phenomenal). Ontologically fundamental.

nodes.name: Logical Possibility Space
nodes.label: W_log (All Logically Possible Worlds)
nodes.description: The set of all logically possible worlds. Includes zombie worlds, inverted qualia worlds, etc.

nodes.name: Natural Possibility Space
nodes.label: W_nat ⊂ W_log (Nomologically Possible)
nodes.description: The set of all naturally (nomologically) possible worlds. Subset of W_log. Constrained by fundamental laws L.

nodes.name: Zombie World
nodes.label: w_z: P(w_z)=P_actual, E(w_z)=∅
nodes.description: A logically possible world where physical facts match actual world but phenomenal experience is absent. Proves logical supervenience fails.

nodes.name: Bridging Function
nodes.label: Ψ: P → E
nodes.description: Psychophysical bridging function mapping physical states to phenomenal states. Implements natural supervenience. Fundamental law in L_Ψ.

nodes.name: Physical Realization
nodes.label: Φ_P: I → P
nodes.description: Physical realization function mapping informational states to physical states. One aspect of dual-aspect monism.

nodes.name: Phenomenal Realization
nodes.label: Φ_E: I → E
nodes.description: Phenomenal realization function mapping informational states to experiential states. Other aspect of dual-aspect monism.

nodes.name: Functional Organization
nodes.label: F_org(x)
nodes.description: Fine-grained functional organization operator extracting causal/functional structure of system x. Basis for organizational invariance.

nodes.name: Structure Operator
nodes.label: S(·)
nodes.description: Structure extraction operator. Extracts relational/geometric structure from domain. Used for structural coherence principle.

nodes.name: Awareness
nodes.label: A ⊂ M
nodes.description: The subset of psychological states M that are directly available for global control and report. Awareness contents, not consciousness itself.

nodes.name: Logical Supervenience P→M
nodes.label: P ⊨_log M
nodes.description: Psychological domain logically supervenes on physical domain. TRUE: M is fully determined by P in all logically possible worlds.

nodes.name: Logical Supervenience P→E
nodes.label: P ⊨_log E
nodes.description: Phenomenal domain logically supervenes on physical domain. FALSE: E is NOT determined by P in all logically possible worlds (zombie argument).

nodes.name: Natural Supervenience P→E
nodes.label: P ⊨_nat E
nodes.description: Phenomenal domain naturally supervenes on physical domain. TRUE: E is determined by P in all naturally possible worlds (via Ψ).

nodes.name: Structural Coherence
nodes.label: S(E) ≅ S(A)
nodes.description: Principle of Structural Coherence: structure of experience isomorphic to structure of awareness. S(E) ≅ S(A).

nodes.name: Organizational Invariance
nodes.label: F_org(x)=F_org(y) ⇒ E(x)≡E(y)
nodes.description: Principle of Organizational Invariance: systems with identical functional organization have identical phenomenal states. Substrate independence.

nodes.name: Double-Aspect Principle
nodes.label: ∀s∈I: ∃Φ_P(s) ∧ ∃Φ_E(s)
nodes.description: Every informational state has both physical and phenomenal realization. Dual-aspect monism: information is neutral substrate.

nodes.name: Causal Closure of Physical
nodes.label: causes(p) ⊆ P
nodes.description: Physical domain is causally closed. All physical effects have sufficient physical causes. No top-down causation from E to P.

nodes.name: Materialism
nodes.label: All facts supervene on P
nodes.description: Thesis that all facts globally logically supervene on physical facts. FALSE in Chalmers framework (refuted by zombie argument).

nodes.name: Property Dualism
nodes.label: E ontologically distinct
nodes.description: Thesis that phenomenal properties are ontologically distinct from physical properties but not separate substance. TRUE in Chalmers framework.

edges.mechanism: Functional realization: psychological states are defined by causal roles fully determined by physical structure
edges.label: P ⊨_log M

edges.mechanism: Zombie argument: logically possible worlds exist where P is identical but E is absent
edges.label: P ⊮_log E (Hard Problem Gap)

edges.mechanism: Bridging function Ψ maps physical states to phenomenal states via psychophysical laws L_Ψ
edges.label: P ⊨_nat E (via Ψ)

edges.mechanism: Psychophysical bridging function Ψ: P → E implements natural supervenience
edges.label: Ψ determines E from P

edges.mechanism: Psychophysical laws L_Ψ define and govern bridging function Ψ
edges.label: L_Ψ grounds Ψ

edges.mechanism: Physical laws determine dynamics and causal structure of physical domain
edges.label: L_P → P dynamics

edges.mechanism: Total laws L include physical laws L_P as subset
edges.label: L_P ⊂ L

edges.mechanism: Total laws L include psychophysical laws L_Ψ as subset
edges.label: L_Ψ ⊂ L

edges.mechanism: Physical realization function Φ_P maps informational states to physical states
edges.label: Φ_P: I → P

edges.mechanism: Phenomenal realization function Φ_E maps informational states to experiential states
edges.label: Φ_E: I → E

edges.mechanism: Physical realization function produces physical states from information
edges.label: Φ_P output

edges.mechanism: Phenomenal realization function produces experiential states from information
edges.label: Φ_E output

edges.mechanism: Structural coherence: structure of experience S(E) isomorphic to structure of awareness S(A)
edges.label: S(E) ≅ S(A)

edges.mechanism: Bidirectional: structure of awareness mirrors structure of experience
edges.label: S(A) ≅ S(E)

edges.mechanism: Structure operator S extracts relational structure enabling coherence verification
edges.label: S enables coherence

edges.mechanism: Existence of zombie world (P identical, E absent) proves logical supervenience fails
edges.label: Zombie → ¬(P ⊨_log E)

edges.mechanism: Zombie worlds demonstrate phenomenal facts do not supervene on physical facts, refuting materialism
edges.label: Zombie → ¬Materialism

edges.mechanism: Failure of logical supervenience of E on P entails materialism is false
edges.label: ¬(P ⊨_log E) → ¬Materialism

edges.mechanism: Bridging function Ψ implements natural supervenience by mapping P to E in all naturally possible worlds
edges.label: Ψ → (P ⊨_nat E)

edges.mechanism: Psychophysical laws ground natural supervenience as nomological necessity
edges.label: L_Ψ → (P ⊨_nat E)

edges.mechanism: Physical states determine functional/causal roles that constitute psychological states
edges.label: P → M (functional)

edges.mechanism: Causal closure ensures all causes of physical events are physical (no top-down causation)
edges.label: Closure → P self-sufficient

edges.mechanism: Functional organization operator enables comparison of different substrates for invariance
edges.label: F_org enables invariance

edges.mechanism: Organizational invariance principle: identical functional organization determines identical phenomenal states
edges.label: F_org(x)=F_org(y) → E(x)=E(y)

edges.mechanism: Information space satisfies double-aspect principle by having dual realizations
edges.label: I has dual aspects

edges.mechanism: Double-aspect principle requires physical realization function
edges.label: Dual aspect → Φ_P

edges.mechanism: Double-aspect principle requires phenomenal realization function
edges.label: Dual aspect → Φ_E

edges.mechanism: Logical possibility space contains zombie worlds as logically possible
edges.label: w_z ∈ W_log

edges.mechanism: Natural possibility space is proper subset of logical possibility space
edges.label: W_nat ⊂ W_log

edges.mechanism: Natural possibility space excludes zombie worlds (Ψ enforces P → E)
edges.label: w_z ∉ W_nat

edges.mechanism: Failure of logical supervenience entails phenomenal properties are ontologically distinct (property dualism)
edges.label: ¬(P ⊨_log E) → Property Dualism

structural_equations.name: Zombie Argument
structural_equations.description: There exists a logically possible zombie world where physical facts match actual world but phenomenal experience is absent

structural_equations.name: Failure of Logical Supervenience
structural_equations.equation: P ⊮_log E ⟺ ¬∀w ∈ W_log : (P(w) = P_actual ⇒ E(w) = E_actual)
structural_equations.description: Phenomenal domain does NOT logically supervene on physical domain

structural_equations.name: Natural Supervenience
structural_equations.description: Phenomenal states are determined by physical states via bridging function Ψ governed by psychophysical laws

structural_equations.name: Logical Supervenience of Psychological
structural_equations.equation: P ⊨_log M ⟺ ∀w ∈ W_log : (P(w) = P_actual ⇒ M(w) = M_actual)
structural_equations.description: Psychological domain logically supervenes on physical domain (functional realization)

structural_equations.name: Structural Coherence
structural_equations.description: Structure of experience isomorphic to structure of awareness

structural_equations.name: Organizational Invariance
structural_equations.description: Identical functional organization entails identical phenomenal states (substrate independence)

structural_equations.name: Double-Aspect Principle
structural_equations.description: Every informational state has both physical and phenomenal realization

structural_equations.name: Causal Closure
structural_equations.description: All causes of physical events are physical (no downward causation from E)

structural_equations.name: Fundamental Laws Decomposition
structural_equations.description: Total laws are union of physical and psychophysical laws; psychophysical laws are NOT derivable from physical laws alone

constraints.name: Zombie Worlds Logically Possible
constraints.description: Zombie worlds (physical duplicates without consciousness) are logically possible

constraints.name: Psychophysical Laws Fundamental
constraints.description: Psychophysical laws cannot be derived from physical laws alone; they are ontologically fundamental

constraints.name: No Downward Causation
constraints.description: Phenomenal states do not cause physical states (causal closure of physical)

constraints.name: Structural Coherence Exact
constraints.description: Structural coherence is exact isomorphism, not mere correlation

constraints.name: Information Space Neutral
constraints.formula: I ≠ P ∧ I ≠ E ∧ (I → P) ∧ (I → E)
constraints.description: Information Space is ontologically prior to and distinct from both physical and phenomenal domains

constraints.name: Natural Worlds Subset
constraints.description: Naturally possible worlds are proper subset of logically possible worlds

constraints.name: Zombie Worlds Naturally Impossible
constraints.description: In all naturally possible worlds, psychophysical laws enforce P → E mapping

expected_outcomes.description: Demonstrates logical independence of phenomenal from physical. Zombie behaves identically but has no experience.
E and P become completely independent. No nomological connection between physical and phenomenal.
Systems with identical functional organization have identical phenomenal states regardless of substrate.

expected_outcomes.explanatory_gap: remains (no account of WHY there is experience)
expected_outcomes.description: Demonstrates irreducibility of E. Can eliminate E from theory but explanatory gap persists.
Chalmers predicts this is naturally impossible. If structure of experience deviates from structure of access, self-reports become unreliable.

expected_outcomes.physics_violation: yes (energy conservation unless via quantum indeterminacy)
expected_outcomes.description: Requires violation of causal closure. Chalmers suggests quantum indeterminacy as only escape route.
